steps.title: Identify the singular antecedent
steps.content: The first sentence introduces "the song 'Danny Boy'" as a singular noun. The second sentence needs a pronoun in the blank to refer back to this song.

steps.title: Apply the pronoun-antecedent agreement rule
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: pronoun-antecedent agreement. Rule: the subject pronoun must match its antecedent in number; the antecedent is the singular noun "the song."
  • Choice A — "those": plural demonstrative pronoun; does not agree with singular "the song." ✗
  • Choice B — "they": third-person plural; does not agree with singular antecedent. ✗
  • Choice C — "each": refers to individual items in a group, not to a single noun; does not serve as a subject pronoun here. ✗
  • Choice D — "it": singular third-person pronoun; correctly refers to the singular antecedent "the song." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
